title: Filament Recycling Cost Comparator
description: Industrial-grade tool to compare the total cost of recycling vs. virgin filament production, incorporating material yield, energy, labor, logistics, and quality-adjusted output. Aligns with ISO 14040/14044 LCA principles and Lean/Six Sigma waste reduction metrics.

inputs.label: Recycling Machine Purchase Cost
inputs.businessContext: Capital expenditure for the recycling extruder/pelletizer system.

businessContext_i18n.en: Capital expenditure for the recycling extruder/pelletizer system.

inputs.label: Machine Useful Life
inputs.businessContext: Expected operational life before major overhaul or replacement.

businessContext_i18n.en: Expected operational life before major overhaul or replacement.

inputs.label: Annual Maintenance Cost
inputs.businessContext: Routine maintenance, spare parts, and service contracts.

businessContext_i18n.en: Routine maintenance, spare parts, and service contracts.

inputs.label: Recycling Energy Consumption
inputs.businessContext: Energy required per kg of recycled filament produced.

businessContext_i18n.en: Energy required per kg of recycled filament produced.

inputs.label: Labor Rate
inputs.businessContext: Hourly wage including benefits for operator.

businessContext_i18n.en: Hourly wage including benefits for operator.

inputs.label: Labor Hours per kg
inputs.businessContext: Direct labor time to process one kg of recycled filament.

businessContext_i18n.en: Direct labor time to process one kg of recycled filament.

inputs.label: Waste Collection Cost
inputs.businessContext: Cost to collect and transport scrap plastic to recycling facility.

businessContext_i18n.en: Cost to collect and transport scrap plastic to recycling facility.

rules.message: Combined yield and quality factor is very low; consider process improvement.
Energy consumption exceeds typical threshold; investigate efficiency.
Low production volume may not justify capital investment.

thresholds.message: Recycling cost exceeds 110% of virgin price; review economics.
Yield below 70% indicates significant material waste.

suggestedActions: Review inputs and verify results against site standards.
